description: The Government Finance Function enables the delivery of high quality public services and ensures that public money is spent efficiently and effectively. GFF is part of the Civil Service and HM Treasury .

details.body:
The Government Finance Function enables the delivery of high quality public services and ensures that public money is spent efficiently and effectively.

GFF is part of the Civil Service and HM Treasury.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Strategy sets out the vision and ambitions of the Government Finance Function (GFF) to 2030.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government Finance Function Strategy 2030

ordered_featured_documents.summary: This is a digital repository of leading practice to support the design of back office operations for finance, HR, grants management and commercial functions.
ordered_featured_documents.title: NOVA Functional Reference Model

image.alt_text: Front cover of Government Efficiency Framework

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The GEF will improve how Departments report efficiencies and ensure the government has appropriate oversight to track delivery and drive improvement.
ordered_featured_documents.title: The Government Efficiency Framework

image.alt_text: Guide cover: magenta background with the GFF logo and text that reads 'Budget holder forecasting handbook'

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The budget holder forecasting handbook sets out why it is important to provide accurate and timely forecasts, what good looks like, and what the benefits are.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Budget holder forecasting handbook

image.alt_text: Cover image for Functional standard for Finance

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The purpose of this government standard is to set expectations for the effective management and use of public funds.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government Functional Standard GovS 006: Finance

details.body: The GIAA Board brings together the Executive Management Team and non-executive board members.

The Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function leads on the governance and strategy of the Government Finance Function, investing in people, sharing best-practice and developing frameworks, guidance and tools to support more than 10,000 people working in finance across government.

role.title: Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: Andrew Cartner - Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function

role.title: Head of the Government Risk Profession

role_appointments.title: Clive Martin - Head of the Government Risk Profession

details.body:
The Chief Operating Officer Group brings together the key functions required to help the business deliver and includes:  

  • commercial 
  • digital, data and technology 
  • finance 
  • human resources  
  • portfolio and project delivery 
  • security, estates, and information 
  • strategy

role.title: Chief Operating Officer, Chief Operating Officer Group

role_appointments.title: Tara Smith - Chief Operating Officer, Chief Operating Officer Group

details.body:
The Chief Operating Officer has overall responsibility for a range of the Department for Business and Trade’s (DBT) corporate functions and support services, including:

  • digital, data and technology
  • people, place and transformation
  • commercial
  • project delivery and change
  • finance
  • security and business services
  • grants delivery

The Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function (GFF) co-leads and develops the community of finance professionals across government, to help to maintain the highest standards of financial management and ensures that public money is spent efficiently and effectively.

Key responsibilities include:

  • setting and delivering a GFF strategy for the development of the Government Finance Function
  • overseeing the professional development and performance of the GFF, including the Government Finance Academy curriculum and the annual continuing professional development plan
  • supporting departments’ talent management, succession planning and recruitment for the most senior financial roles in government

The Joint-Head of the GFF is accountable to Parliamentary select committees, including the Public Accounts Committee, for the continuing improvement of financial management standards, skills and expertise in government.

role.title: Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: Tara Smith - Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

details.body:
The Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function (GFF) co-leads and develops the community of finance professionals across government, to help to maintain the highest standards of financial management and ensures that public money is spent efficiently and effectively.

Key responsibilities include:

  1. Setting and delivering a GFF strategy for the development of the Government Finance Function.
  2. Overseeing the professional development and performance of the GFF, including the Government Finance Academy curriculum and the annual continuing professional development plan.
  3. Supporting departments’ talent management, succession planning and recruitment for the most senior financially qualified roles in government.

The Joint-Head of the GFF is accountable to Parliamentary Select Committees, including the Public Accounts Committee, for the continuing improvement of financial management standards, skills and expertise in government.

role.title: Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: Conrad Smewing - Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • ensuring the delivery of HM Treasury’s work programme, in particular supporting the government’s objectives to ensure effective control of and value for money in public spending and promote sustainable economic growth
  • leading the Treasury’s spending relationship with No 10 and the Cabinet Office
  • supporting spending directors and teams in their relationships with integral Whitehall departments and with the think tank and finance communities

role_appointments.title: Conrad Smewing - Director General, Public Spending

details.body:
Vicky leads the teams responsible for delivering high quality Government financial reporting and data, with a focus on using this information to improve decision making and accountability across government. She also leads the governance and strategy of the Government Finance Function, investing in people, sharing best-practice and developing frameworks, guidance and tools to support more than 10,000 people working in finance across Government.

She is also the Senor Responsible Officer (SRO) for the OSCAR II Project.

ordered_roles.title: Interim Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function

details.body:
Government Finance Function

Mark is a director in the Government Finance Function and leads the GFF’s Risk & Assurance Centre of Excellence work to support key aspects of their priorities covering:

  • Getting the basics right: Sound forecasting and reporting, with robust data, efficient transaction processing, and effective management of risk, supported by standards, policies, guidance and strong functional leadership
  • People, diversity and capability: A high performing and diverse function, with great people in the right roles with the right skills
  • Planning, risk and performance: Driving a strong culture of planning, risk and performance with integrated financial and business planning, aligned with robust risk and assurance

ordered_roles.title: Interim Head of the Government Finance Function
Head of the Government Finance Function
